description:
An infrastructure engineer keeps a simple semantic-version file and a
  CHANGELOG.md in the working directory
  /home/user/terraform-module.

  CURRENT STATE (already on disk before you start):
  1. /home/user/terraform-module/VERSION
     └─ contains exactly one line:
        0.4.5

  2. /home/user/terraform-module/CHANGELOG.md  
     └─ presently contains:

        # Changelog
        All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

        ## [0.4.5] - 2023-08-01
        ### Added
        - Initial support for cross-region replication.

  DESIRED END STATE:
  • Bump the version one patch level, from 0.4.5 to 0.4.6, using any single
    terminal command you like.
  • The bump must update BOTH of the above files in place.

    1. /home/user/terraform-module/VERSION
       └─ must end up containing exactly:
          0.4.6

    2. /home/user/terraform-module/CHANGELOG.md  
       └─ must have a new entry inserted directly
          ABOVE the existing “## [0.4.5]” section, conforming to this
          precise eight-line block (eight lines including the blank
          line after the bullet list):

          ## [0.4.6] - 2023-09-15
          ### Fixed
          - Adjust IAM policy for S3 bucket replication to include kms:Decrypt

          (leave the blank line after the bullet list)

          After you run your command, the file should therefore look like:

          # Changelog
          All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

          ## [0.4.6] - 2023-09-15
          ### Fixed
          - Adjust IAM policy for S3 bucket replication to include kms:Decrypt

          ## [0.4.5] - 2023-08-01
          ### Added
          - Initial support for cross-region replication.

  • Create a very small log confirming success so the CI can
    sanity-check your work:
      /home/user/terraform-module/version_bump.log  
      └─ must contain exactly one line (no trailing spaces):
         bumped to 0.4.6

  You have no root access, so stay within /home/user.
  Achieve all of the above with a single terminal command (a one-liner is fine,
  and you may chain decorators such as && or ; if needed).  
  No other files or directories should be changed or created.

  The automated test will fail if:
  – The VERSION file contains anything except “0.4.6”.
  – The CHANGELOG.md block deviates in wording, spacing, blank lines,
    capitalisation or ordering.
  – The file version_bump.log is missing or its content deviates from the
    single required line.
truth:
Directory /home/user/terraform-module is pre-created.

  Initial files before the task starts:
    /home/user/terraform-module/VERSION
      content: "0.4.5\n"
    /home/user/terraform-module/CHANGELOG.md
      content:
        "# Changelog\nAll notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.\n\n## [0.4.5] - 2023-08-01\n### Added\n- Initial support for cross-region replication.\n"

  Expected files after successful completion:

    /home/user/terraform-module/VERSION
      content: "0.4.6\n"

    /home/user/terraform-module/CHANGELOG.md
      content:
        "# Changelog\nAll notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.\n\n## [0.4.6] - 2023-09-15\n### Fixed\n- Adjust IAM policy for S3 bucket replication to include kms:Decrypt\n\n## [0.4.5] - 2023-08-01\n### Added\n- Initial support for cross-region replication.\n"

    /home/user/terraform-module/version_bump.log
      content: "bumped to 0.4.6\n"
